InterventionNameEnum.description: Intervention names which can be applied to an account
InterventionNameEnum.title: InterventionNameEnum

InterventionCommandEnum.description: Intervention command indicating whether an intervention is to be added or removed from an account
InterventionCommandEnum.title: InterventionUpdateEnum

name.description: Intervention names which can be applied to an account

command.description: Intervention command indicating whether an intervention is to be added or removed from an account

TicfAccountInterventionExtensionsInterve.description: A list of intervention updates to apply to an account
TicfAccountInterventionExtensionsInterve.title: TicfAccountInterventionExtensionsInterventionUpdates

updates.description: a list of updates to make to the interventions on an account

intervention_reason.description: identifies the reason for the intervention, for example, coercion, account-takeover, and so on.

originating_component_id.description: the component id of the application in which the intervention originator, i.e., ticket, case, assessment, etc., that triggered the intervention

originator_reference_id.description: the unique id of the ticket, case, risk assessment, etc., from which the intervention resulted

requester_id.description: the unique id used by the originating component to identify the user who triggered the intervention - will be a number or GUID, must not be personal identifying - MUST be provided if a manual intervention

user_id.description: This is the CommonSubjectID (i.e. the internal version of the pairwiseid) of the user in the relevant part of the system.

description: Triggered when TICF CRI determines an intervention is to be logged/raised

client_id.description: Links the transaction to the client (Relying Party) that initiated the journey.

component_id.description: The component part of the DI system that has generated the event.

event_name.description: A short name, in UPPER_SNAKE format, for the event that has taken place in the system

event_timestamp_ms.description: Records the time and date of when the event occurred. This should be a UTC timestamp and be recorded to the nearest millisecond.

timestamp.description: Records the time and date of when the event occurred. This should be a UTC timestamp and be recorded to the nearest second.

title: TICF_ACCOUNT_INTERVENTION_V2
